notableMoments: Won the 2023 Africa Cup of Nations on home soil, an extraordinary tournament where they came from the brink of elimination to lift the trophy
The golden generation led by Didier Drogba, Yaya Touré, and Kolo Touré qualified for three consecutive World Cups (2006, 2010, 2014) but never advanced past the group stage
Drew in the 'Group of Death' at the 2006 World Cup alongside Argentina, Netherlands, and Serbia & Montenegro, earning respect despite elimination

Didier Drogba's emotional plea for peace in Côte d'Ivoire after qualifying for the 2006 World Cup is one of football's most powerful moments, credited with helping to broker a ceasefire in the civil war
Won the 2015 Africa Cup of Nations in dramatic fashion, with goalkeeper Boubacar Barry saving the decisive penalty in the final against Ghana
The 2023 Africa Cup of Nations triumph under-new-coach represented a new era, with Sébastien Haller, Franck Kessié, and Simon Adingra leading the charge
Qualified for the 2026 World Cup as reigning African champions, looking to finally break through the group stage barrier that eluded their legendary predecessors
The transition from the Drogba-Touré era to the current generation has been one of the most successful rebuilding projects in African football
